title: Historical: Polar.DB USequence reverse load, build, random GetByKey
description: Historical exact-reference experiment based on the earlier Program.cs scenario: create USequence, load one million person records in reverse id order, call Build(), perform one direct GetByKey, then perform 10,000 Random().Next(npersons) GetByKey calls. It intentionally does not include reopen/Refresh.

dataset.notes: Reference generator: Enumerable.Range(0, npersons).Select(i => new object[] { npersons - i - 1, "n" + i, 33.3 }). Age type is PTypeEnumeration.real. Random lookup uses new Random() like the uploaded Program.cs.

workload.notes: Exact historical sequence: Load(reverse ids) -> Build() -> GetByKey(npersons*2/3) -> 10000 random GetByKey. No reopen/Refresh.

fault.notes: No fault injection. This preserves a historical exact-reference regression scenario.

fairness.notes: All Polar.DB targets run the same historical scenario through version-specific typed runners.

runs.notes: Historical scientific profile. CLI may override for quick local runs.
